era: 1984-2000. Psychedelic Rock. E=7 -- thick, heavy, hallucinatory guitar rock. Ellensburg. SST to Epic.
associated_musicians: Jack Endino (Sweet Oblivion co-producer -- connects to Skin Yard at degree 264)
Queens of the Stone Age (Mark Lanegan collaboration)

key_releases.title: Even If and Especially When
key_releases.label: SST
key_releases.significance: SST debut. Underground arrival. Lanegan's voice as primary instrument.

key_releases.title: Buzz Factory
key_releases.label: SST / Sub Pop
key_releases.significance: Final SST record. Most aggressive Trees album.

key_releases.title: Sweet Oblivion
key_releases.label: Epic
key_releases.significance: Major-label debut. 'Nearly Lost You.' Don Fleming + Jack Endino production. Commercial peak.

key_releases.title: Dust
key_releases.label: Epic
key_releases.significance: Artistic peak. Barrett Martin's complex drumming. Post-grunge maturity.

theory_nodes.concept: ELLENSBURG ENTERS THE ENGINE. Degree 261 is the first eastern Washington city in the S36 engine. The Cascade Range is crossed. Ellensburg: Kittitas Valley, 1,500 ft elevation, high desert climate, 100 miles from Puget Sound via I-90. The Trees' slightly out-of-time, isolated-sounding psychedelia has a geographic logic.
theory_nodes.description: First eastern WA entry. Engine geography expands across the Cascades.

theory_nodes.concept: Mark Lanegan's baritone as primary instrument. The deep fractured baritone -- not a technique but a condition. The voice carries weight and authority that contemporaries could not replicate. Born Ellensburg 1964, died Killarney Ireland 2022, age 57. Post-Trees career: QOTSA collaborator, Gutter Twins, solo albums through Blues Funeral (2012). One of the irreplaceable voices in American rock.
theory_nodes.description: Lanegan's baritone: the primary element of the Trees' identity.

theory_nodes.concept: SST-to-Epic arc. Screaming Trees formed on SST Records' ideas about independent rock before grunge was a commercial category. Three SST records (1987-1989) established the band pre-commercially. Epic signing (1991) brought them into the grunge moment without losing artistic identity. The crossing from underground independence to major-label presence: one of the most intact of any PNW band.
theory_nodes.description: SST (underground) to Epic (major label): the Trees' full label arc.

theory_nodes.concept: Jack Endino connection: Sweet Oblivion (261) co-produced by Endino. Endino also produced Nirvana's Bleach, Mudhoney's Superfuzz Bigmuff, and is the bandleader of Skin Yard (degree 264). The Endino thread runs through the entire proto-grunge and grunge network.
theory_nodes.description: Endino thread: degree 261 (production) to degree 264 (Skin Yard member).

cross_references.relationship: Storm Arc closes (E=9 peak). Engine steps to E=7 at 261. Geophysics Arc opens.
Jack Endino co-produced Sweet Oblivion (261) and is Skin Yard's guitarist/bandleader (264). The Endino thread.
E=7 holds. Psychedelic Rock to Garage Rock. Ellensburg to Fremont.

sps_pair.pairing_note: GEOPHYSICS ARC OPENS. Both operate below the ordinary register: Lanegan's baritone below the usual vocal range; volcanic tremor below the usual acoustic range. Underground forces in the landscape, underground voice in the music. E=7 + E=5.
